initial_posts.narrative: As a senior data scientist in the financial sector, I see NAIS 2.0 as a major push to accelerate AI adoption. The policy commits over S$500 million for AI R&D, which aligns with the resource logic stating 'Over S$500 million committed through AI Singapore for AI R&D.' The co-funding cap of S$330,000 per 100E project is explicitly stated. For compute, I estimate about 500,000 GPU hours based on typical allocations for national AI programmes of this scale—enough to support dozens of experiments. Trainer personnel count is harder to pin down, but given the ambition to train 15,000 practitioners, I'd expect at least 50 dedicated trainers and AI experts to guide enterprises and practitioners, drawing from academia and industry.

The resource logic clearly states 'Co-funding up to S$330,000 per project for 100 Experiments,' so max_co_funding_per_project_sgd is 330,000.

To support the target of boosting the AI practitioner pool to 15,000, a team of 50 trainers (each handling multiple cohorts) is a plausible initial staffing level.
